title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtim
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders include end users (homeowners/tenants) prioritizing portability (<1.5 kg), adequate runtime (>=20 min), effective cleaning performance, low noise, easy maintenance, and convenient charging via a base. Secondary users may include people with limited strength requiring ergonomic handling and balanced center of gravity. Market/regulatory stakeholders require compliance with applicable EU safety and product requirements (e.g., electrical safety, EMC, battery safety, chemicals/material restrictions, labeling/documentation). Retailers/distributors care about durability, returns, packaging, and clear claims substantiation (runtime, weight). Service/operations stakeholders care about battery life cycle, filter replacement availability, cleaning/emptying process, and charging safety. Engineering/manufacturing stakeholders need clear targets for suction/airflow, dust capacity, battery chemistry/capacity, charge time, noise, reliability, and cost constraints, plus environmental conditions (indoor 10–40°C) and use cases (quick pickups vs whole-room).
clarification_questions: What cleaning performance targets should be met (e.g., intended debris types/surfaces, minimum suction power/airflow, and dustbin capacity)?

What are the charging and battery requirements beyond a plug-in base (e.g., max full charge time, replaceable vs non-replaceable battery, expected cycle life, and runtime definition—normal vs max mode)?

What constraints apply for noise, price/cost target, and key EU compliance scope (e.g., specific directives/standards you want explicitly covered such as CE, RoHS/REACH, WEEE, EMC, battery transport/safety)?
